stories.headline: Mindanao quake death toll climbs to 47 as recovery efforts continue across affected provinces
stories.summary: The death toll from the magnitude 7.8 earthquake that struck off Sarangani on June 8 rose to 47, with 688 people injured and 31 still missing, according to the latest official tallies cited by the Philippine Daily Inquirer. More than 75,000 families have been displaced and over 1,300 public schools across six regions were damaged. PHIVOLCS has recorded more than 3,000 aftershocks, including a magnitude 5.5 jolt in Davao Occidental on the morning of June 11.
stories.context: The June 8 quake off Sarangani was among the strongest recorded in Mindanao in decades and briefly prompted tsunami warnings that were later lifted.

stories.headline: Bipartisan group of senators visits quake-hit Sarangani to deliver aid
stories.summary: Senate President Pro Tempore Sherwin Gatchalian led a group of senators, including Erwin Tulfo, JV Ejercito and Migz Zubiri, on a visit to earthquake-affected areas in Sarangani province and General Santos City on June 11, the Philippine Daily Inquirer reported. The senators distributed about P2.5 million in cash assistance and sacks of rice and met with affected residents. Gatchalian said the trip was meant to determine residents' needs firsthand, and Zubiri said the assistance crossed political lines.
stories.watch_for: Senator Erwin Tulfo said he would coordinate with the Department of Public Works and Highways on a review of the quality of infrastructure in the quake-affected region.

stories.headline: Marcos announces P50,000 aid for families of quake victims, P100 million for General Santos
stories.summary: During a visit to General Santos City on June 10, President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. said the government would give P50,000 to each family that lost a member in the magnitude 7.8 Mindanao earthquake, according to the Manila Bulletin. He said General Santos City would receive P100 million from the Local Government Support Fund for repairs to its city hall, with additional funds to be released for Sarangani and South Cotabato. Marcos said power supply in affected areas was about 85 percent restored.
stories.watch_for: Marcos said workers displaced by the quake and those affected by business closures would be assisted through Department of Social Welfare and Development and Department of Labor and Employment programs.

stories.headline: Senate sets June 18 pre-trial as Gatchalian affirms 16 votes needed to convict VP Duterte
stories.summary: Acting Senate President Sherwin Gatchalian said the threshold to convict Vice President Sara Duterte in her impeachment trial remains 16 votes, or two-thirds of the 24-member Senate, the Manila Bulletin reported. He said the figure is unaffected by the dispute over Senate leadership, in which his bloc counts 12 senators and a rival bloc led by Alan Peter Cayetano counts 10. The impeachment court set a pre-trial conference for June 18.
stories.context: The trial follows months of political division, with rival Senate blocs disputing who legitimately leads the chamber and will preside over the proceedings.
stories.watch_for: The Duterte camp and the House prosecution panel were directed to submit pre-trial briefs by June 15, with the trial proper scheduled to begin July 6.

stories.headline: Analysts say sustained oil pressure could push BSP toward further rate hikes
stories.summary: MUFG Bank said elevated global oil prices, aggravated by Middle East tensions, could push the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas to raise interest rates further to support the peso, the Manila Bulletin reported. Senior currency analyst Lloyd Chan said the Philippines is among the net oil importers most exposed to currency pressure. MUFG projected up to 75 basis points of additional hikes by end-2026, lifting the policy rate to 5.25 percent from 4.5 percent. May inflation eased to 6.8 percent from April's 7.2 percent but stayed above the 2 to 4 percent target.
stories.context: The peso has traded near record lows against the dollar amid the conflict, and the BSP raised its key rate in April for the first time after nearly two years of easing.

stories.headline: Agencies subpoena Ateneo officials in probe of basketball players' drowning deaths
stories.summary: The Department of the Interior and Local Government said it subpoenaed the coaches and players present when Ateneo de Manila basketball players Rene Baterbonia and Divine Adili drowned during a June 8 team-building activity in Dipaculao, Aurora, GMA News reported. Barangay officials, hotel staff and the team's bus driver were also summoned. The PNP Criminal Investigation and Detection Group is examining possible Anti-Hazing Act violations, while the NBI is looking into possible criminal negligence. Aurora police earlier found no indication of foul play.
stories.watch_for: Those summoned were directed to appear on June 15. The Department of Labor and Employment separately subpoenaed head coach Tab Baldwin to verify his work permit.

stories.headline: Quake-damaged hospitals in Sarangani treat patients in outdoor tents
stories.summary: Hospitals in earthquake-hit Sarangani have been treating patients in outdoor tents after the magnitude 7.8 quake left buildings unsafe, GMA News reported. At Sarangani Provincial Hospital, staff treated patients outside and at one point delivered a baby in a makeshift area, while a facility in Glan had more than 60 patients in beds set up outdoors. A collapsed bridge and damaged roads left some communities reachable only by helicopter. Office of Civil Defense regional director Rodrigo Sosmena said rescuers were proceeding cautiously because of continuing aftershocks.

stories.headline: PAGASA says southwest monsoon to bring rains to western Northern Luzon
stories.summary: The state weather bureau PAGASA said the southwest monsoon, or habagat, would bring cloudy skies with scattered rains and thunderstorms to the western section of Northern Luzon, including Ilocos Norte, Apayao, Batanes and the Babuyan Islands. It warned that heavy rainfall could trigger flash floods or landslides in those areas. The rest of the country, including Metro Manila, would see partly cloudy to cloudy skies with isolated rain showers or thunderstorms, according to its forecast issued on the afternoon of June 11.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes after helicopter downed near Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The United States carried out strikes on targets in southern Iran after President Donald Trump said Iran had downed a US Army Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz, Al Jazeera reported. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said it retaliated with drone and missile attacks on US-linked military sites in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan; its claim of destroying F-35 hangars could not be independently verified. The exchanges strained a fragile ceasefire reached earlier in the year. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Tehran would leave no attack unanswered.
stories.context: The flare-up is part of a months-long US-Israel confrontation with Iran; renewed tension over the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil shipping route, has helped drive up global oil prices.
